what_this_should_exercise: F1 CoxPH drivers — strong signals: Contract Length (Monthly=100% churn), Support Calls, Tenure, Payment Delay
F2 stat panel — churn_by_plan_tier + churn_by_support_volume + churn_by_usage_decile + tenure buckets all populated
F3 self-consistency — 3 Gemini runs at temps 0.2/0.5/0.7 vote on causes
F4 per-cause RAG — query expansion for each suspected cause
F5 top_segments — Basic plan + Monthly contract should dominate
F9 strategy_skeptic — adversarial review of strategies
F10 RAG-anchored simulation — lift priors from retention frameworks
F11 evidence_dossier — stat → cause → tactic chain
F13 HyDE — hypothetical answer for 'first 90 days' segment
F14 competitor_research — fires because churn_destination = HubSpot is in KNOWN_COMPETITORS

questionnaire.business_context: Subscription-based digital service with three plan tiers (Basic / Standard / Premium) and three contract cadences (Monthly / Quarterly / Annual). Customers self-onboard, billing is automated, support is mixed CSM + self-serve. We've grown to ~440k customers historically and currently retain a working sample of ~20k for diagnostic analysis.
questionnaire.market_segment: Working professionals aged 28-48, primarily individual subscribers and small teams, evaluating value monthly and quick to cancel after a single bad billing cycle or support friction event.
edge_cases: Monthly contract subscribers are churning at near-100% — we suspect this is partly definitional (the dataset labels them all churn=1 once their contract ends) but want strategies that still treat short-cadence subscribers as a real cohort.
questionnaire.typical_customer: Working professionals aged 28-48, primarily individual subscribers and small teams, evaluating value monthly and quick to cancel after a single bad billing cycle or support friction event.
